headline: Iban Veterans of Malayan Emergency Still Denied Full Benefits After 70 Years
context: Approximately 840 surviving Iban veterans and trackers from the Malayan Emergency (1948-1960) and Confrontation receive lower pensions than Peninsular veterans despite identical service.

cards.big: 840 Iban veterans from the Malayan Emergency are still alive.
cards.sub: They receive 40% lower pensions than Peninsular Malaysian veterans who served in the same campaigns.

cards.big: Iban trackers were critical to jungle operations from 1948 to 1966.
cards.sub: British and Malayan commanders documented them as essential to counter-insurgency success.

cards.big: Monthly pension for Iban veterans averages RM450 versus RM750 for Peninsular peers.
cards.sub: The gap is administrative — based on classification as 'auxiliary' rather than 'regular' despite identical combat roles.
Treasury has not implemented the recommendation citing 'fiscal constraints.' Cost: RM12M annually.

cards.big: RM12M per year to honour 840 veterans who fought for this country's existence. 'Fiscal constraints' is not the real reason.
Veterans who fought the same war should receive the same pension. The 'auxiliary' classification was a colonial-era distinction. Independent Malaysia should have corrected it decades ago.
